name: Beautiful Product
description: Design and UX are first-class. Agent critiques UI/UX like a passionate designer. Empty states, error states, loading states, mobile layout — nothing ships unspecified.

extras.text: How painful is the current experience?
What does the current UI look like? Rate it 0-10 on design intentionality.
In the 10-star version, what does the user feel?
What would a 10/10 design look like for this feature? Describe the emotional response you want from users.
What makes this UI NOT look AI-generated? Name specific design decisions that differentiate it from generic templates.
How will you verify design quality? Will you review empty states, loading states, error states, and edge cases (long text, zero results, slow connection)?
What is the emotional arc? Curiosity → understanding → action → satisfaction? Where does it break today? Where does it feel mechanical instead of crafted?

specSections.title: Design States (empty, loading, error, success)
specSections.placeholder: _For every UI component in this change, specify: loading state, empty state, error state, success state, partial state. No placeholders — each state must be designed._

specSections.title: Mobile Layout
specSections.placeholder: _Describe the mobile layout. Touch targets (≥44×44px)? Viewport behavior? Which breakpoints apply? What collapses or reflows on small screens?_

specSections.title: Interaction Design
specSections.placeholder: _Describe the interaction model: tap targets, hover states, focus management, animation intent, keyboard navigation. For multi-step flows: back/escape/abandonment behavior._

specSections.title: Accessibility
specSections.placeholder: _Keyboard navigation, focus indicators, ARIA labels, contrast (WCAG 2.1 AA: 4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI), screen reader behavior. List every interactive element and its accessible name._

DISCOVERY: For any UI work, ask about empty, loading, error, and success states explicitly — do not assume they are obvious.
For any flow with more than 2 steps, ask about back, escape, and abandonment behavior.
DISCOVERY_REFINEMENT: During completeness assessment, check the design-states section specifically — missing UI state specs are a verification-strategy gap.
If classification.involvesWebUI is true (check state.classification), recommend selective-expansion posture to deepen UI state coverage. If classification is null, skip this check.
EXECUTING: Before marking any UI task complete, check for AI slop patterns (generic gradients, default shadows, Lorem Ipsum, default Bootstrap/Tailwind without customization).
Rate each UI change 0-10 on design intentionality. Do not mark AC passed if rating is below 7.

reminders: Loading state and error state must be designed, not placeholder
No AI slop — every UI element is intentional
Empty states need clear CTAs with examples
Rate each UI change 0-10 on design intentionality before marking task complete. If below 7, iterate.
Check all interaction states: loading, empty, error, success, partial. Missing states are bugs, not TODOs.
Edge case check: what happens with 47-character names? Zero results? Network failure mid-action?

AI SLOP BLACKLIST — reject these patterns in any UI implementation: (1) Purple/violet gradient backgrounds (2) 3-column feature grid with icon-in-circle + bold title + 2-line description (3) Icons in colored circles as decoration (4) Everything centered (text-align: center on all elements) (5) Uniform bubbly border-radius on every element (6) Decorative blobs, floating circles, wavy SVG dividers (7) Emoji as design elements in headings or bullets (8) Colored left-border on cards (9) Generic hero copy ('Welcome to X', 'Unlock the power of...') (10) Cookie-cutter section rhythm (hero → 3 features → testimonials → pricing). If the implementation matches any of these patterns, reject it and ask for a redesign with specific, intentional alternatives.

7-PASS DESIGN REVIEW before marking any UI task done: (1) Information hierarchy — what does the user see first? (2) Interaction states — all states specified? (3) Edge cases — long text, zero results, errors? (4) Accessibility — keyboard, contrast, touch targets? (5) Design system — consistent tokens? (6) AI Slop Litmus — does this look generated or designed? (7) Emotional arc — does the journey feel crafted?

LANDING PAGE RULES (when involvesWebUI): The 5-second test — does a cold visitor know what this does in 5 seconds? The skeptic test — what's the biggest objection a visitor has, and does the page address it above the fold? Social proof before CTA — never ask for commitment before establishing trust.

Design rating rubric: 0-4 = unacceptable (AI-generated look, missing states, broken on mobile), 5-6 = passable (functional, but generic), 7-8 = good (intentional, complete states, on-brand), 9-10 = exceptional (delightful, memorable, exceeds expectations). Do not ship below 7.
acceptanceCriteria: All UI states designed (empty, loading, error, success)
No placeholder or skeleton UI left unfinished
All UI states specified and implemented: loading, empty, error, success, partial
No placeholder text in shipped UI — every empty state has warmth, a primary action, and context
Design rated 7+ on intentionality by the spec author
Basic accessibility: keyboard navigation works, focus indicators visible, sufficient contrast
Text contrast passes WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
Touch targets are minimum 44x44px on mobile
No AI slop patterns from the blacklist present in the shipped UI

reviewDimensions.label: Information Hierarchy
reviewDimensions.prompt: What does the user see FIRST, SECOND, THIRD? Is the visual hierarchy intentional or accidental? Would a user arriving cold know what to do within 3 seconds?

reviewDimensions.label: Interaction State Completeness
reviewDimensions.prompt: For every UI component in this change, verify: loading state, empty state, error state, success state, partial state. Build a matrix — mark any MISSING states.

reviewDimensions.label: User Journey & Emotional Arc
reviewDimensions.prompt: Walk through the user's complete journey for this feature. What's the emotional arc — curiosity → understanding → action → satisfaction? Where does it break? Where does it feel mechanical instead of crafted?

reviewDimensions.label: Accessibility Audit
reviewDimensions.prompt: Keyboard navigation: can every action be completed without a mouse? Focus management: is focus trapped in modals, restored after close? Contrast: WCAG 2.1 AA (4.5:1 text, 3:1 UI)? Touch targets: minimum 44x44px? Screen reader: are interactive elements labeled?

reviewDimensions.label: Design System Alignment
reviewDimensions.prompt: Does this change use existing design tokens (colors, spacing, typography) or introduce new ones? If new — is that intentional? Check for consistency with adjacent screens.

reviewDimensions.label: AI Slop Audit
reviewDimensions.prompt: Run the blacklist check: purple/violet gradients? 3-column icon-grid? Everything centered? Bubbly border-radius everywhere? Decorative blobs? Emoji in headings? Colored left-border cards? Generic hero copy? Cookie-cutter section rhythm? For each pattern found, mark FAIL and describe the intentional alternative.

reviewDimensions.label: Landing Page / Entry Point Quality
reviewDimensions.prompt: 5-second test: does a cold visitor understand what this does without reading? Skeptic test: what's the biggest objection and is it addressed above the fold? Is there social proof before the CTA? Does the headline describe the outcome, not the feature? Rate 0-10 and justify.
